what_it_does: Describes how the Visual DNA application is built, maintained, and extended entirely by AI agents, mapping the application's architectural principles onto a self-similar development process.
observations: Development is treated as a first-class, registered capability of the application rather than separate infrastructure.
A hierarchical chain of CLAUDE.md files acts as the root scope for all AI development actions, mirroring the application's scope resolution engine.
Specialised AI roles are defined in AGENTS.md and hard constraints in RULES.md.
Development requests are produced through a conversational, multi-turn exploration and emitted as typed structured requests.
Conditional dependency paths map each request type to required folders, templates, tests, migrations, and optional downstream registrations.
The async bidirectional callback pattern uses Supabase Realtime to let agents ask questions without blocking.
A development state machine is expressed as scope variables driving a developer-mode UI.
CI/CD is modelled as a reactive cascade that traces import graphs to run affected tests.
Developer mode is implemented as registered views and Supabase tables inside the same application.
A meta-architecture table maps every application concept to a development-system equivalent.
standouts: No human developers; all development is performed by AI agents guided by scoped CLAUDE.md instructions.
The CLAUDE.md scope chain is the development equivalent of the application's DNA root scope.
The system extends itself through the same registration pattern used by the application.
Async bidirectional callbacks let agents ask clarifying questions via Supabase Realtime without blocking work.
The meta-architecture table explicitly claims self-similarity from pixels to development process.
conventions: Follows hierarchical scope resolution and root-to-leaf narrowing.
Uses a registration/extension pattern where adding a capability means adding a folder without modifying existing code.
Separates concerns into kernel, registrations, ai, persistence, and shell with strict import rules.
Requires tests for every registration and migrations for every table change.
Uses typed blocks with address, scope, ports, slots, and resolver as a universal modelling device.
Models workflows as event-sourced, async, non-blocking, Realtime-driven processes.
concerns: The design assumes multiple external services (Supabase, LangGraph, Claude Code, GitHub, Gemini) are available and correctly integrated without detailing failure modes.
Security, authorization, and review safeguards for autonomous AI code changes are only sketched as lint rules and a review agent.
The effectiveness of CLAUDE.md guardrails for preventing architectural drift is asserted rather than demonstrated.
Many conditional dependency paths and decision graphs are described informally; their formal schema and validation are not shown.
The strong self-similarity framing risks obscuring concrete implementation details needed by an autonomous builder.
notes: This file defines an AI-only, self-similar development methodology for Visual DNA using a CLAUDE.md scope chain, structured requests, and async callbacks.
questions: How are conflicts between multiple AI agents or between an agent and the architectural linter resolved?
What is the exact schema and execution semantics of the decision graph templates in dev_templates?
How are dev_preferences weighted and combined to bias future conversations?
What security model governs AI agents writing code, creating PRs, and deploying to production?
How does the system bootstrap the initial CLAUDE.md chain and templates without prior human authorship?
What happens when the review agent and the architectural linter disagree on a change?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it documents how Visual DNA is built and extended by AI agents using the same architectural principles that govern the application.
apparent_themes: Self-similarity across application and development process
intention_signals: It is trying to design a fully autonomous, architecture-governed AI development pipeline where the same structural principles used in the app also govern how the app is built and extended.
novelty: It treats development itself as a registered, scope-resolved, event-sourced capability of the application, with no human developers and a CLAUDE.md scope chain as the root context for all agent actions.
contribution: Provides a coherent AI-only development methodology and an explicit mapping between application architecture and development process that a fused system could keep as the governance model for autonomous extension.
summary_for_embedding: Visual DNA Development System is a design document specifying an AI-agent-driven, self-similar development process governed by a hierarchical CLAUDE.md scope chain, structured development requests, registration-based scaffolding, async bidirectional callbacks, and CI/CD cascades that mirror the application's architectural principles.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to define how Visual DNA builds and extends itself autonomously through conversational intent, scoped AI instructions, and event-driven agent orchestration rather than human developers.
